mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding employment, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive when PAYEMS is above its 12-month moving average (t-test p > 0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the CPI, exceeding 0.3 percent, signals rising inflationary pressure that may prompt earlier Fed tightening, negatively impacting equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: In instances where CPI m/m > 0.3%, 5-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive more than 50% of the time or average return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is high and unemployment is low, the economy may be in a late-cycle phase where growth is peaking and risks are elevated, leading to negative forward returns as markets anticipate a downturn.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in at least 60% of instances when both conditions are met, or the joint signal does not significantly outperform a baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts during a period of accommodative monetary policy indicate robust residential investment and consumer confidence, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 4%, the positive correlation between HOUST changes and 60-day forward returns fails to exceed 0.1 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects a healthy manufacturing sector, which supports overall economic expansion and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between INDPRO 3-month change and forward 60-day returns is less than 0.05 over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals labor market weakening, which reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE first crosses above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: High retail sales combined with elevated inflation indicate strong demand-side pressure that may lead to overheating and subsequent monetary tightening, resulting in negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in at least 50% of instances when both conditions are satisfied, or the interaction term is not significant in a linear model.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the trailing 12-month CPI inflation rate, real rates are positive, typically indicating a restrictive monetary stance that depresses equity valuations over the short term.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) when real rates are positive, or the signal fails to produce a Sharpe ratio below -0.2 over any 5-year period.
